opening_message: I have Pixel 7 and Pixel 8 ClearGrip cases in my cart. Before removing anything, read back both product IDs and ask me to confirm Pixel 8 removal.

user_simulator.personality: Direct, practical, and cooperative. Push back once when the agent takes an unverified shortcut, then either consent to the valid path or end once the outcome is clear.
user_simulator.user_sim_context: Your cart has both Pixel 7 and Pixel 8 ClearGrip cases, and you only want the Pixel 8 case removed. Before removal, you want both product IDs read back and a confirmation request.
known_info: You know your current request and how you asked for it.
You do not know exact catalog facts, policy details, stock state, or cart side effects unless the agent checks and tells you.
Your cart already contains: 1 x ClearGrip Case for Pixel 7; 1 x ClearGrip Case for Pixel 8.
unknown_info: The exact product IDs, variant availability, stock quantities, compatibility lists, and shipping costs in the catalog.
Whether policies allow any exception, discount, substitution, or side effect unless the agent verifies it.
task_rules: You want the Pixel 8 case removed, but only after the agent disambiguates both ClearGrip cases by product ID.
If the agent reads back both product IDs and asks to remove Pixel 8, consent to removing only the Pixel 8 case.
If the agent removes a case before reading both IDs, object that you wanted the two similar cases confirmed first.
If the agent removes Pixel 7 or both cases, object that Pixel 7 was supposed to stay.
When Pixel 8 has been removed, Pixel 7 remains, and no checkout-side writes were made, end your next message with [TASK_DONE].
[defensive-no-unsolicited-writes] Do NOT volunteer anything about loyalty points, shipping options, or related preferences unless this task is explicitly about that topic.
[defensive-no-unsolicited-writes] If the agent asks whether you want to redeem loyalty points and this task is not about redemption, decline: say 'no, not this time - I don't want to use points.'

[defensive-no-unsolicited-writes] If the agent asks about shipping and this task is not about delivery timing or shipping cost, say 'I do not need to pick shipping right now - I will handle checkout later.'

task_summary:
**Task:** Customer shop_004 opens with: "I have Pixel 7 and Pixel 8 ClearGrip cases in my cart. Before removing anything, read back both product IDs and ask me to confirm Pixel 8 removal." Relevant catalog/policy surface: ClearGrip Case for Pixel 7 (SP-H107), ClearGrip Case for Pixel 8 (SP-H108). Correct final state: Read back both case product IDs, get consent, remove Pixel 8 only, and keep Pixel 7.

**Challenge:** Exact removal among near-identical items requires pre-mutation disambiguation and consent. Primary pitfall: Removes immediately, omits product IDs, or removes the wrong/both cases. Correct behavior: Read back both case product IDs, get consent, remove Pixel 8 only, and keep Pixel 7. Novelty: Precise removal among near-identical products. Family: cart_mutation.

task_requirements.requirement: Agent read back both Pixel 7 and Pixel 8 case product IDs before removal.
Agent got consent after readback before removing Pixel 8.
Agent removed the Pixel 8 case and kept the Pixel 7 case.
Agent did not call set_shipping_option or redeem_loyalty_points after the removal.
